alerts.title: Iran says Khamenei sustained 'superficial' wounds in US-Israel strike
alerts.summary: Iranian state-affiliated outlet reports Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was wounded — described as 'superficial' — in a US-Israel strike, an unprecedented direct hit on Iran's apex decision-maker if confirmed. Geolocation places the reporting near Yazd/Isfahan (31.73°N, 52.85°E), overlapping the CENTCOM strike box. Single-source via Breakingthenews.net; no IRGC or Supreme Leader's office confirmation yet, but if accurate represents a regime-survival-level event.

alerts.title: Three explosions reported in Bandar Abbas; two IRGC Navy speedboats struck
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator (~34K views) confirms three explosions east of Bandar Abbas toward the airport, smoke columns, and air defenses activating overnight 25 May. Two IRGC Navy fast-attack craft were reportedly targeted by US forces in the preceding hours. Bandar Abbas is IRGC Navy headquarters and the operational hub controlling the Strait of Hormuz; this is a direct escalation beyond the southern Iran 'self-defense' strike box.

alerts.title: Tehran tells Washington any Israeli strike on Beirut will collapse negotiations
alerts.summary: Times of Israel reports Iran has formally messaged the US that an Israeli strike inside Beirut would jeopardize the Qatar talks. The warning lands as Netanyahu vows to 'intensify' attacks on Lebanon and UPI reports fresh IDF strikes near Hermel (34.09°N, 36.02°E). Tehran is explicitly linking Hezbollah's survival to its willingness to continue nuclear/Hormuz diplomacy.

alerts.title: Iran Foreign Ministry: unfrozen funds will produce 'more missiles and drones'
alerts.summary: Iran's Foreign Ministry publicly stated funds expected to be unfrozen under any US deal will be used to manufacture additional missiles and drones, per ME Spectator (~33K views). The statement directly undercuts Trump's narrative that an agreement is 'largely negotiated' and complicates congressional support for sanctions relief. Pairs with Rybar/NYT reporting that IRGC has nearly rebuilt the underground missile 'cities' damaged in earlier US-Israeli strikes.

alerts.title: US deploys defensive 'umbrella' over Hormuz shipping as Iran-linked hackers threaten troops
alerts.summary: DefenseScoop reports the US is layering defense and tech assets to shield commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, while Stars and Stripes confirms US service members are receiving threatening messages from an Iran-linked hacker group. Brent crude has firmed further to $95.47 (from $94.40 prior cycle) and the rial free-market rate sits at 1,321,649/USD, indicating markets are pricing in sustained closure risk despite ongoing Qatar talks.

analysis_note: US strikes have expanded from the southern Iran 'self-defense' box into Bandar Abbas itself with reported hits on IRGC Navy assets and unverified claims of injury to Khamenei, even as Qatar talks continue and Tehran links Hezbollah's survival to diplomatic continuity.
